title: pyrrocoma calendulacea
intro: Pyrrocoma calendulacea, also known as California rubber plant, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Pyrrocoma calendulacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating various skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat skin irritations, burns, and wounds.
